commit.message:
fix: replace 1000-block sled scan with in-memory reward counter to prevent RPC stall

The watchdog was restarting nodes because get_pool_reward_tracking() was
scanning up to 1000 blocks from sled synchronously on the async runtime
thread — blocking the RPC accept loop for 1-3+ seconds per block.

Changes:
- Add update_reward_counters(): O(n) in-memory counter update called from
  add_block; persists rewarded nodes immediately and all nodes every 10
  blocks via sled_insert_bg (non-blocking background writer)
- Add get_reward_tracking_from_memory(): O(n) read-lock, replaces the
  1000-block sled scan in produce_block_at_height, validate_pool_distribution,
  and the main.rs block-production loop
- Add reconstruct_reward_counters(): O(n) startup recovery from persisted
  last_reward_height, called after blockchain init so counters are accurate
  from block 1 without any sled scan

This eliminates the RPC stall that was causing busy_streak to escalate
to fail_streak and trigger watchdog restarts.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
[Repo Assist] eng: add Dependabot for GitHub Actions + v3 OperationCompiler unit tests (#364)

* eng: add dependabot for GitHub Actions + v3 operation compilation tests

Task 4 (Engineering Investments):
- Add .github/dependabot.yml to enable weekly Dependabot alerts for
  GitHub Actions version updates (checkout, setup-dotnet, cache).

Task 9 (Testing Improvements):
- Add Schema.OperationCompilationTests.fs with 9 unit tests covering
  the v3 OperationCompiler pipeline:
  - Simple GET generates a correctly named method
  - No-parameter GET has CancellationToken as sole parameter
  - Task<string> return type for JSON string response
  - Required params come before optional ones in method signature
  - CancellationToken is always last regardless of param count
  - POST with body: body param precedes CancellationToken
  - POST with 201/no-body returns Task<unit>
  - CT name deduplication when a query param is named cancellationToken
  - Multiple operations each get their own CancellationToken param

All 272 unit tests pass (0 failures, 2 pre-existing skips).
Format verified with Fantomas.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor: extract shared compileV3Schema helper to Schema.TestHelpers.fs

Resolves #2004: Optimize activation code generation — DB changeset rework (#2311)

* Resolves #2004: Rework DB changeset and add performance test scripts

- Reorder changeset operations: NOT NULL first (fail-fast), then unique
  constraint (expensive index build), then drop old index
- Add NOT NULL constraint on activation_code to align DB schema with
  the JPA entity definition (discrepancy since Liquibase introduction)
- Add comment explaining NULL safety for compound unique constraint
- Add performance test SQL scripts for PostgreSQL, Oracle and MS SQL
  to measure index build time on 10M rows

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Resolves #2004: Fix Oracle perf scripts, add NULL retrofit changeset and README

- Fix Oracle pa_activation_perf_test.sql: replace INSERT /*+ APPEND */ with
  batched FORALL loop to fix silent data generation failure in PL/SQL context;
  add EXCEPTION handler for visibility
- Fix Oracle pa_activation_perf_test_create_db.sql: remove invalid GRANT CREATE
  INDEX privilege; add ALTER SESSION SET CONTAINER = FREEPDB1 for correct PDB
  context; update connection string examples
- Fix Oracle pa_activation_perf_test_drop_db.sql: add ALTER SESSION SET
  CONTAINER = FREEPDB1
- Add 20260316-activation-code-fix-nulls.xml: Liquibase NULL retrofit changeset
  for legacy DBs with preConditions onFail=MARK_RAN (no-op on clean databases)
- Update db.changelog-version.xml: include fix-nulls changeset before unique
  constraint changeset
- Add docs-private/db/README.md: unified performance comparison across
  PostgreSQL 17, Oracle 19c, Oracle 23ai Free, MSSQL 2022, Azure SQL Edge
  on 10M-row migration benchmark
- Add docs-private/db/mssql/docker-compose.yml: Docker Compose for MSSQL
  (Azure SQL Edge ARM64-native) for local testing on Apple Silicon

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add Oracle docker-compose.yml for local testing

Add docs-private/db/oracle/docker-compose.yml using gvenzl/oracle-free:latest
(Oracle 23ai Free) with native ARM64 support. PDB: FREEPDB1.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add PostgreSQL docker-compose.yml for local testing

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Update README: PostgreSQL 18.2, add macOS/ARM64 test environment notes

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Remove docker-compose.yml files from docs-private/db

commit.message:
feat(sdk): namespace improvements for deepagents (#2386)

# Namespace factory refactor

This PR changes `StoreBackend`'s `namespace` factory to receive a
`Runtime` instance directly instead of a `BackendContext` wrapper.
Factories now look like `lambda rt: (rt.server_info.user.identity,
"fs")` rather than `lambda ctx: (ctx.runtime.context.user_id, "fs")`.

Backwards compatibility is preserved via `_NamespaceRuntimeCompat`,
which duck-types as both `Runtime` and the legacy `BackendContext` and
emits `DeprecationWarning`s when `.runtime` or `.state` is accessed.
`BackendContext` and the old-style callable signature are deprecated and
will be removed in v0.7. Tests across the backends and middleware suites
were updated to the new signature.

docs update: https://github.com/langchain-ai/docs/pull/3496/changes

fix(alerter): honor configured query concurrency (#1111)

* fix(alerter): honor configured query concurrency

Honor the configured alerter concurrency when throttling query execution.

A reported scheduling drift bug showed alert rules running well past their configured interval under load because workers were still gated by a hard-coded queue depth of 5, even when the alerter was configured for higher concurrency.

Create the worker queue from the configured concurrency and pass it through the executor to each worker. Expanding the queue this way helps due rules start closer to their specified schedule when many queries become runnable at once, while preserving the historical fallback for callers that do not set concurrency.

Refs: alert scheduling drift bug report

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(alerter): respect cancellation while waiting for slots

Avoid blocking indefinitely on query slot acquisition during shutdown by selecting on ctx.Done() while waiting for a slot.

Also make the default-concurrency assertion use queue.DefaultConcurrency and add a regression test covering canceled slot acquisition.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(alerter): simplify worker query slots

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(alerter): tighten query slot regressions

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

all its per-name arrays — net savings proportional to unique symbol
count in the project.

commit.message:
fix: set API ingress to INGRESS_TRAFFIC_ALL

Cloud Run-to-Cloud Run calls over the public internet URL are treated as
external traffic. INGRESS_TRAFFIC_INTERNAL_ONLY was blocking all requests
from the UI BFF and returning 404.

Access control is enforced via IAM: osrs-ui-sa holds roles/run.invoker
on the API service. Cloud Run IAM auth rejects unauthorized callers at
the load balancer before the container is invoked.

commit.message:
Deploy: 更新博客内容 2026-04-10 21:53:07

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(auth): add Express middleware for JWT auth, disable Wonderwall autoLogin

Replace Wonderwall autoLogin with Express-level JWT middleware to allow
M2M traffic from KISS while enforcing auth on all other routes.

Middleware behavior:
- Health checks (/api/isalive, /api/isready): pass through
- M2M routes (/api/v1/*): pass through (own introspection-based auth)
- Valid user JWT: pass through (validates signature, issuer, audience, expiry)
- App tokens (idtyp=app): rejected on non-M2M routes (defense-in-depth)
- Browser requests without token: redirect to /oauth2/login
- API requests without token: 401 Unauthorized
- Development without NAIS cluster: pass through

NAIS config: autoLogin: true → false

121 new tests: 23 middleware unit tests + 98 per-endpoint enforcement tests.
Reviewed by GPT 5.4, Codex, Sonnet, and Opus (2 rounds).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Harden CI gates and AI reliability

Restore Python coverage gate to 70%, expand AI-service test coverage to 78%, fix evaluator CLI output generation, broaden CI smoke tests, and stabilize Playwright CI runtime path.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: Add Docker Compose configurations and improve tenant logging

- Add docker-compose.local.yml for local development with PostgreSQL + pgAdmin
- Add docker-compose.prod.yml for production with external database
- Improve Dockerfile: Use Alpine base, better MongoDB installation
- Enhance TenantController logging for better debugging
- Add improved error messages in API responses
- All containers start successfully and API endpoints respond correctly

Local setup verification:
✅ Health endpoint: /api/health
✅ Login endpoint: /api/central/login
✅ Create tenant: /api/tenants (POST)
✅ List tenants: /api/tenants (GET)

Known issues to fix in next PR:
- Tenant migrations not running automatically (relations not created)
- Need to debug seeding process

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: resolve 13 security vulnerabilities across all tools

Critical:
- Replace pickle.loads() with JSON serialization in embed.py (arbitrary code exec)
- Set config file permissions to 0o600 and prefer env vars for API keys

High:
- Fix SQL injection: parameterized queries in query-session.py
- Fix TOCTOU race condition: atomic lock creation in watch-sessions.py
- Fix path traversal: validate WSL paths in sync-knowledge.py

Medium:
- FTS5 query injection: sanitize special chars/operators (query-session.py, briefing.py)
- Silent error handling: add stderr logging to ~23 bare except blocks
- Input validation: length limits for titles, content, and filters
- Whitelist validation for f-string DDL in migrations
- Database integrity check (PRAGMA quick_check) on open
- Backup verification with SHA-256 checksums

Added:
- test_security.py: 9 security verification tests
- SECURITY.md: security policy and best practices

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: copilot-session-knowledge
repository.description: Turn your AI coding sessions into a searchable knowledge base. FTS5 + semantic vector search for GitHub Copilot CLI.

commit.message:
ci: add NODE_AUTH_TOKEN to npm publish step

The publish step was missing NODE_AUTH_TOKEN, causing authentication
failures when 2FA is required on the npm account. actions/setup-node
configures .npmrc to read NODE_AUTH_TOKEN, but the env var must be
explicitly passed to the step.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: visible marker delete + arrow key scrubbing

- MarkerList: remove opacity-0 from delete button so it is always
  visible (was hidden until hover, unusable on mobile/touch)

- editor.vue: global keydown listener for arrow key scrubbing
  ← / → = seek ±5 seconds
  Shift+← / Shift+→ = seek ±1 second
  Ignored when an input/textarea has focus

- Timeline: update keyboard hint to show ← → scrub + M marker

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
